title: Granadilla suberosa
intro: Granadilla suberosa, also known as Pouteria suberosa or Cuatrocienegas tree, is a tropical plant species native to the Americas, characterized by its unique, warty, and deciduous trunk. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Granadilla suberosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Granadilla suberosa is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Granadilla suberosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It belongs to the genus Malpighia, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the malpighia family. Within the genus Malpighia, Granadilla suberosa is a distinct species due to its unique characteristics and features. The classification of Granadilla suberosa is further supported by its morphological and anatomical features, which distinguish it from other species within the genus. The classification of Granadilla suberosa is also based on its phylogenetic relationships with other species in the family Malpighiaceae.
